Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1320a, , as added , , , provided for assistance in the form of institutional services in intermediate care facilities, the subsecs. providing as follows: subsec. (a), modification of certain plans to include such benefit; subsec. (b), eligible individuals; subsec. (c), payments and Federal medical assistance percentage; subsec. (d), conditions, limitations, rights, and obligations applicable to modified plans; and subsec. (e), definition of “intermediate care facility”, which is covered in , prior to repeal by , , .act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 531, title XI, § 1121Pub. Time Periods for Establishment of Uniform Reporting Systems; Consultations With Interested Parties, , , directed Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare to establish the systems described in subsec. (a) of this section only after consultation with interested parties and for hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, and intermediate care facilities, not later than the end of the one year period beginning on , and for other types of health services facilities and organizations, not later than the end of the two-year period beginning on .Pub. children.heading: Establishment; criteria for regulations; requirements for hospitals
children.label: a Establishment; criteria for regulations; requirements for hospitals

children.text: The aggregate cost of operation and the aggregate volume of services.
The costs and volume of services for various functional accounts and subaccounts.
Rates, by category of patient and class of purchaser.
Capital assets, as defined by the Secretary, including (as appropriate) capital funds, debt service, lease agreements used in lieu of capital funds, and the value of land, facilities, and equipment.

children.heading: Monitoring, etc., of systems by Secretary
children.label: b Monitoring, etc., of systems by Secretary

children.text: monitor the operation of the systems established under subsection (a);
assist with and support demonstrations and evaluations of the effectiveness and cost of the operation of such systems and encourage State adoption of such systems; and
periodically revise such systems to improve their effectiveness and diminish their cost.

children.heading: Availability of information to appropriate agencies and organizations
children.label: c Availability of information to appropriate agencies and organizations
children.text: The Secretary shall provide information obtained through use of the uniform reporting systems described in subsection (a) in a useful manner and format to appropriate agencies and organizations, including health systems agencies (designated under section 300–4 of this title) and State health planning and development agencies (designated under section 300m of this title), as may be necessary to carry out such agencies’ and organizations’ functions.l11See References in Text note below.1
